title: MCQ – Chapters 1 to 12 Mixed (200 Questions)
source: Short Textbook of Anesthesia – Ajay Yadav (6th Edition) – Chapters 1–12
section: Anatomy, Equipment, Airway, Preop, Difficult Airway, Monitoring, Fluids, History, GA Intro, Inhalational, Gases, IV Anesthetics

questions.question: Which is the narrowest part of the airway in children up to 6 years?
questions.explanation: Subglottis (at the level of cricoid) is the narrowest part in children up to the age of 6 years.

questions.question: Propofol is a potent:
questions.explanation: Propofol is a potent antiemetic agent.

questions.question: Magill circuit is the circuit of choice for:
questions.explanation: Magill circuit is the circuit of choice for spontaneous ventilation.

questions.question: Capnography is the surest sign to confirm:
questions.explanation: Capnography is the surest sign to confirm the correct position of endotracheal tube.

questions.question: For solid food, the fasting period before surgery is:
questions.explanation: For solid food 8 hours of fasting is must while clear fluids can be given up to 2 hours.

questions.question: Mallampati grading, thyromental distance and neck movements are assessed in:
questions.explanation: Mallampati grading, thyromental distance and neck movements are the three most important parameters to be assessed in every case.

questions.question: Invasive blood pressure monitoring is considered as:

options.B: Gold standard monitoring of blood pressure

questions.explanation: Invasive blood pressure monitoring is considered as gold standard monitoring of blood pressure.

questions.question: Ringer lactate is the most preferred fluid for:
questions.explanation: Ringer lactate is the most preferred fluid for maintenance as well as replacement.

questions.question: First public demonstration of ether was given by WTG Morton on:
questions.explanation: First public demonstration of ether was given by WTG Morton on 16th October 1846.

questions.question: The basic components (TRIAD) of general anesthesia are:
questions.explanation: The basic components (TRIAD) of general anesthesia are Narcosis, Analgesia, Muscle relaxation.

questions.question: Which inhalational agent is the most potent among agents used nowadays?
questions.explanation: Halothane with MAC of 0.74% is the most potent.

questions.question: Histotoxic hypoxia does NOT respond to:
questions.explanation: Oxygen therapy is helpful in all types of hypoxia except histotoxic hypoxia.

questions.question: During general anesthesia with muscle relaxants, the cords are held in:
questions.explanation: During general anesthesia with muscle relaxants the cords are held in cadaveric position.

questions.question: Etomidate is the most cardiovascular stable among all:
questions.explanation: Etomidate is the most cardiovascular stable among all IV agents.

questions.question: The color of oxygen cylinder is:
questions.explanation: The color of oxygen cylinder is black body with white shoulders (top) and pressure is 2,000 psi.

questions.question: Among second generation LMA, which is most frequently used in India?
questions.explanation: Among the second generation LMA, I-Gel is most frequently used in India.

questions.question: MAO-A inhibitors should be stopped before surgery:
questions.explanation: MAO-A inhibitors need to be stopped 3 weeks before surgery.

questions.question: Every head injury should be considered to be having cervical injury until:
questions.explanation: Every head injury should be considered to be having cervical injury until proved otherwise.

questions.question: The most preferred leads for ECG monitoring are:
questions.explanation: The most preferred leads for ECG monitoring are lead II and V5.

questions.question: DIC is the most common cause of death after:
questions.explanation: DIC is the most common cause of death after massive transfusion.

questions.question: The word anesthesia meaning 'no senses' was coined by:
questions.explanation: The word anesthesia was coined by Oliver Wendell Holmes.

questions.question: Preoxygenation with 100% oxygen for 3 minutes replaces 99% of nitrogen in lungs. This is called:
questions.explanation: 99% of nitrogen in lungs can be replaced with oxygen (called as Denitrogenation) in 3 minutes.

questions.question: Blood gas partition coefficient of desflurane is:
questions.explanation: Blood gas partition coefficient of desflurane is 0.42, making it fastest induction/recovery among agents in use.

questions.question: The critical temperature of oxygen is:
questions.explanation: The critical temperature of oxygen is –119°C.

questions.question: Aspiration in supine position most commonly involves:

options.B: Posterior basal segment of right lower lobe
options.C: Apical segment of right lower lobe

questions.explanation: In supine position most commonly involved segment is apical segment of right lower lobe.

questions.question: Ketamine is the most potent analgesic among:
questions.explanation: Ketamine is the most potent analgesic among IV agents.

questions.question: Bain circuit is the circuit of choice for:
questions.explanation: Bain circuit is the circuit of choice for controlled ventilation.

questions.question: Fiberoptic laryngoscope-guided intubation is considered as gold standard for:
questions.explanation: Fiberoptic laryngoscope/bronchoscope-guided intubation is considered as gold standard technique for the management of difficult/failed intubation.

questions.question: Clopidogrel should be stopped before surgery:
questions.explanation: Clopidogrel should be stopped 5 days prior to surgery.

questions.question: Intubation success rates with video laryngoscopy after failed direct laryngoscopy:
questions.explanation: Intubation success rates can be as high as 94–99% with video laryngoscopy after failed intubation with direct laryngoscopy.

questions.question: The maximum interval between two BP recordings should not exceed:
questions.explanation: The maximum interval between two blood pressure recordings should not exceed more than 5 minutes.

questions.question: One unit of blood/packed cells increases hemoglobin by:
questions.explanation: One unit of blood/packed cells increases hemoglobin by 0.8%.

questions.question: First local anesthetic used was cocaine by Carl Koller for anesthetizing:
questions.explanation: First local anesthetic used was cocaine by Carl Koller for anesthetizing cornea (1884).

questions.question: The stage of surgical anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: Stage of surgical anesthesia is stage III plane 3 because laryngeal reflexes goes in this stage.

questions.question: MAC of sevoflurane is:
questions.explanation: MAC of sevoflurane is 2.05%.

questions.question: Medical oxygen is prepared by:
questions.explanation: Medical oxygen is prepared by fractional distillation of air.

questions.question: Maximum airway resistance to airflow occurs in:
questions.explanation: Maximum airway resistance to airflow occurs in trachea and then main bronchus and minimum in terminal bronchi.

questions.question: The elimination half-life of Thiopentone is 10.4 hours but consciousness is regained after 15-20 minutes due to:
questions.explanation: Consciousness is regained after 15–20 minutes because of redistribution.

questions.question: Entonox is a mixture of:
questions.explanation: Entonox is the mixture of 50% oxygen and 50% nitrous oxide.

questions.question: Macintosh is the most commonly used:
questions.explanation: Macintosh is the most commonly used laryngoscope.

questions.question: Smoking should ideally be stopped before surgery:
questions.explanation: Smoking should ideally be stopped 8 weeks before surgery.

questions.question: For cervical spine injury, intubation should be done with neck in:
questions.explanation: For cervical spine injury intubation should be done after neck stabilization with neck in neutral position.

questions.question: Oxygen saturation of mixed venous blood is best guide to assess:
questions.explanation: Oxygen saturation of mixed venous blood is best guide to assess tissue perfusion (cardiac output).

questions.question: Platelets are the only blood products stored at:
questions.explanation: Platelets are the only blood products which are stored at room temperature.

questions.question: First anesthesia machine was invented by Edmund Gaskin Boyle in:
questions.explanation: First anesthesia machine was invented by Edmund Gaskin Boyle in 1917.

questions.question: Loss of consciousness is tested by absence of which reflex?
questions.explanation: First reflex to go is eyelash, therefore loss of consciousness is tested by absence of eyelash reflex.

questions.question: Sevoflurane can produce Compound A with:
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane by reacting with sodalime and Barylime can produce compound A.

questions.question: Cyanosis appears when reduced Hb level is more than:
questions.explanation: Cyanosis is produced if reduced Hb level is more than 5 g/dL.

questions.question: P50 (partial pressure at which O2 saturation is 50%) is:
questions.explanation: P50 is not affected by anesthetics.

questions.question: Remifentanil is the opioid of choice for:
questions.explanation: Remifentanil is the opioid of choice for day care surgery.

questions.question: Jackson Rees (Mapleson F) is the most commonly used circuit for:
questions.explanation: Jackson Rees (Mapleson F) is the most commonly used semiclosed circuit for children.

questions.question: The chief advantage of PVC endotracheal tube is:
questions.explanation: The chief advantage of PVC tube is that their cuff is low pressure and high volume.

questions.question: ACE inhibitors and angiotensin II antagonists — what should be done on surgery day?

options.B: Only morning dose to be omitted

questions.explanation: ACE inhibitors and angiotensin II antagonist — only morning dose to be omitted.

questions.question: Thyromental distance less than 6 cm indicates:
questions.explanation: Thyromental distance < 6.0 cm: Laryngoscopy may be impossible.

questions.question: BIS (Bispectral Index) score indicating adequate depth of anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: Bispectral index score (BIS) score of 45–60 indicates adequate depth.

questions.question: Crystalloids should be replaced in ratio to intravascular volume loss:
questions.explanation: Crystalloids should be replaced in a ratio of 1.5:1 to intravascular volume loss.

questions.question: First IV anesthetic used in clinical practice was thiopentone by Water and Lundy in:
questions.explanation: First intravenous anesthetic used was thiopentone by John Lundy in 1934.

questions.question: In current day practice, most anesthetists use which gas instead of nitrous oxide for maintenance?
questions.explanation: Majority of anesthesiologists in current day practice use air instead of nitrous oxide.

questions.question: Isoflurane is the inhalational agent of choice for:
questions.explanation: Isoflurane is the agent of choice for cardiac patients.

questions.question: 100% oxygen is usually considered safe for normal adults up to:
questions.explanation: 100% oxygen is usually considered safe up to 8–12 hours in normal adults.

questions.question: Closing capacity is normally how much less than functional residual capacity?
questions.explanation: Closing capacity is normally 1 L less than functional residual capacity.

questions.question: Midazolam is the only benzodiazepine used in:
questions.explanation: Midazolam is the only BZ used in intraoperative period.

questions.question: Pin index system prevents wrong fitting of:
questions.explanation: Pin index system is to prevent the wrong fitting of cylinders.

questions.question: Heat and moisture exchanger (HME) is the most commonly used device to preserve:
questions.explanation: Heat and moisture exchanger (HME) is the most commonly used device to preserve humidity in anesthesia.

questions.question: Warfarin should be stopped before surgery:
questions.explanation: Oral anticoagulants (Warfarin) should be stopped 4 days prior to surgery.

questions.question: In 'cannot intubate, cannot ventilate' situation, the emergency procedure is:
questions.explanation: Cricothyroidotomy which should be replaced by definitive measure (tracheostomy) as soon as possible.

questions.question: Train of four ratio > 0.9 guarantees:
questions.explanation: Train of four ratio > 0.9 guarantees recovery.

questions.question: Most common cause of mismatch transfusion is:
questions.explanation: Most common cause of mismatch transfusion is ABO incompatibility.

questions.question: Harold Griffith was first to use which muscle relaxant?
questions.explanation: Harold Griffith was first to use 'curare' for muscle relaxation (1942).

questions.question: The last reflex to go during anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: The last reflex to go is carinal reflex.

questions.question: Desflurane is the agent of choice for all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Desflurane has unpleasant induction (coughing, breath-holding) making it unsuitable for induction in children.

questions.question: Nitrous oxide is stored in blue cylinders at pressure of:
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide is stored in blue color cylinders at a pressure of 760 psi.

questions.question: Venturi principle is utilized in anesthesia particularly with:
questions.explanation: Venturi principle is often utilized in anesthesia particularly with Venturi masks.

questions.question: All pressures (intraocular, intragastric, intracranial) are raised by:
questions.explanation: All pressures like intraocular, intragastric, intracranial are raised by ketamine.

questions.question: Upper end of bobbin determines the flow rate in:
questions.explanation: Upper end of bobbin determines the flow rate in rotameter.

questions.question: During laryngoscopy, the position should be extension at atlanto-occipital joint and:
questions.explanation: During laryngoscopy, there should be extension at atlanto-occipital joint and flexion at cervical spine.

questions.question: Herbal medications should be stopped before surgery:
questions.explanation: All herbal medications should be stopped 7 days before surgery.

questions.question: Mallampati Grade IV means only visible structure is:
questions.explanation: Class IV: Only hard palate is visible.

questions.question: Radial artery is most commonly chosen for cannulation because it has:
questions.explanation: As radial artery has collaterals (transpalmar arch) for the blood supply of hand, it is most commonly chosen artery for cannulation.

questions.question: The main aim of blood transfusion is to:
questions.explanation: The main aim of blood transfusion is to improve the oxygen delivery of tissues, not the volume replacement.

questions.question: Nitrous oxide was first synthesized by Priestley in:
questions.explanation: First anesthetic gas, nitrous oxide, was synthesized by Priestley in 1774.

questions.question: Succinylcholine is the ideal muscle relaxant for intubation because of:
questions.explanation: Due to its rapid onset, suxamethonium (succinylcholine) is the ideal muscle relaxant for intubation.

questions.question: Nitrous oxide can deplete ozone layer and produce:
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide can deplete ozone layer and produce global warming.

questions.question: Oxygen concentrators produce oxygen from ambient air by selective absorption of nitrogen on:
questions.explanation: Oxygen concentrators produce oxygen from ambient air by selective absorption of nitrogen on zeolites.

questions.question: Endotracheal tubes decrease anatomical dead space by:
questions.explanation: Endotracheal tubes and tracheostomy decrease the anatomical dead space by bypassing the upper airways.

questions.question: Propofol is the IV agent of choice for:
questions.explanation: Propofol is the IV agent of choice for day care surgery.

questions.question: Aladin cassette vaporizer can be used to deliver:
questions.explanation: Aladin cassette vaporizer is the latest vaporizer, which can be used to deliver all inhalational agents.

questions.question: The most common cause of hypoxia for double lumen tube is:
questions.explanation: The most common cause of hypoxia for double lumen tube is ventilation perfusion mismatch.

questions.question: In current day practice, premedication is given with:
questions.explanation: In the current day practice of anesthesia patient is premedicated with a purpose not as a routine procedure.

questions.question: Cormack and Lehane Grade III laryngoscopy means:
questions.explanation: Grade III: Only epiglottis visible.

questions.question: PAOP is best utilized to differentiate between:
questions.explanation: PAOP is best utilized to differentiate between cardiogenic and noncardiogenic pulmonary edema (ARDS).

questions.question: Colloids should be replaced in ratio to intravascular volume loss:
questions.explanation: Colloids are replaced in 1:1 ratio.

questions.question: John Snow popularized chloroform and used it on:
questions.explanation: John Snow used chloroform on Queen Victoria for birth of her 8th child.

questions.question: Suxamethonium use in routine intubation is restricted because of:
questions.explanation: Because of the side effects its use is only restricted to rapid sequence or difficult intubation.

questions.question: Sevoflurane is the agent of choice for induction in:
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane is the agent of choice for induction in children.

questions.question: Half-life of CO at 1 atm is 214 minutes, reduced to 19 minutes at:
questions.explanation: Half-life of CO at 1 atm is 214 minutes which can be reduced to 19 minutes at 2.5 atm.

questions.question: During general anesthesia, alveolar dead space is:
questions.explanation: During general anesthesia alveolar dead space is increased.

questions.question: The major side effect of etomidate is:
questions.explanation: The major side effect of etomidate is adrenocortical suppression.

questions.question: Diameter index safety system prevents wrong fitting of:
questions.explanation: Diameter index safety system is to prevent the wrong fitting of central supply pipe lines.

questions.question: Fixed performance (high flow) devices deliver:
questions.explanation: Fixed performance devices deliver accurate oxygen concentration.

questions.question: High dose estrogen oral contraceptives should be stopped before surgery:
questions.explanation: High dose estrogen oral contraceptives should be stopped 4 weeks prior to surgery.

questions.question: Airway assessment failure can make anesthetist:
questions.explanation: Airway assessment failure can make anesthetist negligent in case of some catastrophe.

questions.question: Each degree (°C) fall in temperature reduces metabolic rate by:
questions.explanation: Each degree (°C) fall in temperature reduces the metabolic rate by 6 to 7%.

questions.question: Normovolemic/isovolemic hemodilution is the most widely used technique for:
questions.explanation: Normovolemic/isovolemic hemodilution is the most widely used technique for autologous blood donation.

questions.question: First endotracheal intubation was done by:
questions.explanation: First endotracheal intubation was done by Ivan Magill.

questions.question: First reflex to come back during recovery from anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: Theoretically cough should come first but swallowing comes earlier because coughing also requires diaphragm effort.

questions.question: Halothane is absolutely contraindicated in:
questions.explanation: Halothane is contraindicated in pheochromocytoma as it sensitizes myocardium to catecholamines.

questions.question: Nitrous oxide vitamin B12 inactivation is seen if used for more than:
questions.explanation: Vitamin B12 inactivation is seen if nitrous oxide is used for more than 12 hours.

questions.question: All inhalational agents blunt hypoxic pulmonary vasoconstriction thereby increasing:
questions.explanation: All inhalational agents blunt HPV response thereby increasing the shunt fraction.

questions.question: Sufentanil is the agent of choice for inhibiting stress response to:
questions.explanation: Sufentanil is the agent of choice for inhibiting stress response to laryngoscopy and intubation.

questions.question: Fresh gas flow for Bain circuit during controlled ventilation is:
questions.explanation: Fresh gas flow for controlled ventilation is 1.6 times of minute ventilation.

questions.question: The most common airway used to prevent tongue fall is:
questions.explanation: The most common airway used to prevent the tongue fall is Guedel's airway.

questions.question: Clear fluids can be given up to how many hours before surgery?
questions.explanation: Clear fluids can be given up to 2 hours before surgery.

questions.question: Normal thyromental distance is:
questions.explanation: Thyromental distance Normal: 6.5 cm or more (> 3 finger breadth).

questions.question: Hypothermia is the most common thermal perturbation seen during:
questions.explanation: Hypothermia is the most common thermal perturbation seen during anesthesia.

questions.question: Children are prone for fluid overload therefore should receive:
questions.explanation: Children are prone for fluid overload therefore should receive 2/3rd of the calculated dose.

questions.question: Humphry Davy called nitrous oxide as:
questions.explanation: Humphry Davy used nitrous oxide and called it 'laughing gas'.

questions.question: Induction is accomplished with IV anesthetics because of their:
questions.explanation: Intravenous agents are preferred for induction because of their ultrafast action (within 15 seconds).

questions.question: Desflurane at concentrations > 1 MAC (>6%) stimulates:
questions.explanation: At concentrations more than 1 MAC (>6%) desflurane stimulates sympathetic system.

questions.question: Retrolental fibroplasia with oxygen toxicity occurs in premature neonates less than:
questions.explanation: Premature neonates (< 36 weeks) are at greatest risk for retrolental fibroplasia.

questions.question: Due to shorter, wider and less acute angle, endobronchial intubation is more common on:
questions.explanation: Due to shorter, wider and less acute angle chances of endobronchial intubation are more on right side.

questions.question: Emergence reactions (hallucinations) are the major limiting factor for:
questions.explanation: Emergence reactions, mainly hallucinations, are the major limiting factor for restricting the use of ketamine.

questions.question: Amsorb neither produces compound A nor carbon monoxide with:
questions.explanation: Amsorb neither produces compound A nor carbon monoxide with desiccated sodalime and Barylime.

questions.question: The major limitation of mask ventilation is increased chances of:
questions.explanation: The major limitation of mask ventilation is that it increases the chances of aspiration.

questions.question: Thrombolytic therapy — patient can be considered for elective surgery after:
questions.explanation: Patients can be considered for elective surgery 10 days after the last dose of thrombolytics.

questions.question: In anticipated difficult intubation, the preferred technique is awake intubation with:
questions.explanation: If difficult airway is anticipated then intubate in awake state preferably with fiberoptic bronchoscope.

questions.question: Normal CVP is:
questions.explanation: Normal CVP is 3–10 cm of H2O.

questions.question: Blood can be stored for 35 days with preservative:
questions.explanation: Blood can be stored for 35 days if CDPA-1 is used.

questions.question: First spinal anesthesia in human beings was given by:
questions.explanation: First spinal anesthesia in human beings was given by August Beir in 1898.

questions.question: During anesthesia with inhalational agents, expiration is active mediated by:
questions.explanation: During anesthesia with inhalational agents expiration is active, mediated by abdominal muscles.

questions.question: MAC of desflurane is:
questions.explanation: MAC of desflurane is 6.0%.

questions.question: Paul Bert effect (convulsions after hyperbaric oxygen) is usually seen above:
questions.explanation: Paul Bert effect is usually seen if the pressure used is > 3 atm.

questions.question: FRC (Functional Residual Capacity) during general anesthesia decreases by:
questions.explanation: During general anesthesia FRC decreases by 15–20%.

questions.question: Thiopentone is absolutely contraindicated in:
questions.explanation: Thiopentone is absolutely contraindicated in acute intermittent and variegate porphyria.

questions.question: Desiccated sodalime can cause burns of respiratory mucosa with sevoflurane by producing:
questions.explanation: Desiccated sodalime can cause burns of respiratory mucosa with sevoflurane by producing hydrogen fluoride.

questions.question: Most commonly used video laryngoscope is:
questions.explanation: Most commonly used video laryngoscopes is C-MAC.

questions.question: Disulfiram should be discontinued before surgery:
questions.explanation: Disulfiram to be discontinued 10 days before the surgical procedure.

questions.question: Inter incisor gap (mouth opening) should be at least:
questions.explanation: Inter incisor gap (mouth opening) should be at least 5 cm (2 finger breadth).

questions.question: Ideal vein for CVP monitoring is:
questions.explanation: Ideal vein for monitoring of central venous pressure is right internal jugular vein.

questions.question: Blood products should NOT be mixed with Ringer lactate because it contains:
questions.explanation: As RL contains calcium blood should not be given through the same drip set (calcium with citrate can induce clot formation).

questions.question: Ether was first prepared by Valerius Cordus in 1540 known as:
questions.explanation: Ether was prepared by Valerius Cordus in 1540 when it was known as 'sweet oil of vitriol'.

questions.question: The components of balanced anesthesia include all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Components of balanced anesthesia are Narcosis, Analgesia, Muscle relaxation, Amnesia, Abolition of reflexes, and Maintenance of normal hemodynamics.

questions.question: Halothane hepatitis carries mortality of more than:
questions.explanation: Halothane hepatitis carries very high mortality (>70%).

questions.question: Nitrous oxide is how many times more soluble than nitrogen?
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide is 35 times more soluble than nitrogen.

questions.question: Normal tidal volume is:
questions.explanation: Tidal volume is 400–500 mL (10 mL/kg).

questions.question: Injection of propofol is:
questions.explanation: Injection of propofol is painful.

questions.question: Fail safe valve cuts off or decreases flow of nitrous oxide when:
questions.explanation: Fail safe valve in case of decreased oxygen pressure either cut-off or proportionately decreases the flow of nitrous oxide.

questions.question: Definitive airway management devices include endotracheal tube and:
questions.explanation: Definitive airway management devices includes endotracheal tube and tracheostomy.

questions.question: Viagra should be stopped before surgery:
questions.explanation: Viagra to be stopped 24 hours prior to surgery.

questions.question: In 'cannot intubate but can ventilate' situation, Plan A is:
questions.explanation: Consider reattempt intubation called as plan A (maximum 4 times) with manipulations.

questions.question: Electrical impedance pulmonometry is simplest method to detect apnea in:
questions.explanation: Electrical impedance pulmonometry is the simplest and most commonly used method to detect apnea in a non-intubated patient.

questions.question: The most acceptable trigger point for blood transfusion is hemoglobin less than:
questions.explanation: The most acceptable trigger point for blood transfusion is hemoglobin < 8 gm%.

questions.question: First epidural anesthesia was given by:
questions.explanation: First epidural anesthesia was given by Cathelin and Sicard (1901).

questions.question: Loss of consciousness during IV induction occurs in arm-brain circulation time of:
questions.explanation: Unconsciousness is produced in one arm brain circulation time, i.e. 15 seconds.

questions.question: Methoxyflurane can produce:
questions.explanation: Methoxyflurane can produce vasopressin-resistant polyuric renal failure.

questions.question: The most common type of hypoxia seen during anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: Hypoxic hypoxia is the most common type of hypoxia seen during anesthesia.

questions.question: Normal anatomical dead space is:
questions.explanation: Anatomical dead space is normally 30% of tidal volume or 2 mL/kg or 150 mL.

questions.question: Dexmedetomidine is a new:
questions.explanation: Dexmedetomidine is a new α2 agonist which is frequently used in anesthetic practice.

questions.question: Oxygen flush delivers oxygen at:
questions.explanation: Oxygen flush delivers 35 liters of oxygen per minute at a pressure of 45–60 psi.

questions.question: Second generation LMA has significantly decreased incidence of:
questions.explanation: Second generation LMA has shown significant decrease in incidence of aspiration.

questions.question: Glycopyrrolate is preferred over atropine because it does not cross:
questions.explanation: Glycopyrrolate does not cross the blood-brain barrier therefore is devoid of central side effects.

questions.question: Neck circumference >43 cm is associated with:
questions.explanation: Short neck with circumference >43 cm is a cause of difficult intubation.

questions.question: Co-oximeters can differentiate between:
questions.explanation: Co-oximeters are special types of oximeters which can differentiate between normal and abnormal hemoglobin.

questions.question: Hydroxyethyl starch at doses >20 mL/kg can interfere with:
questions.explanation: At high doses (>20 mL/kg) hydroxyethyl starch can interfere with clotting and can cause renal dysfunction.

questions.question: 'Balanced anesthesia' concept was laid by John Lundy in:
questions.explanation: Balanced anesthesia, a concept laid by John Lundy in 1926.

questions.question: After discontinuing nitrous oxide, 100% oxygen should be given for:
questions.explanation: 100% oxygen should be given for 5–10 minutes after discontinuing nitrous oxide to prevent diffusion hypoxia.

questions.question: In neonates, 100% oxygen for more than how many hours can cause pulmonary toxicity?
questions.explanation: In infants and neonates 100% oxygen for more than 2–3 hours can cause pulmonary toxicity.

questions.question: Tolerance is seen to all actions of opioids EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Tolerance is seen to all actions of opioids except constipation and miosis.

questions.question: Pulse oximetry works on the principle of:
questions.explanation: Pulse oximeters work on the principle of Beer-Lambert law.

questions.question: Maximum breathing capacity is:
questions.explanation: Maximum breathing capacity is 120–170 L/min.

questions.question: Ketamine is the induction agent of choice for shock, full stomach, and:
questions.explanation: Ketamine is the induction agent of choice for shock, full stomach, low cardiac output states and right to left shunts.

questions.question: Sodalime composition: Ca(OH)2 is:
questions.explanation: Standard traditional composition of sodalime: Ca(OH)2 is 94%.

questions.question: Current day anesthetic practice is an era of:
questions.explanation: Current day anesthetic practice is an era of supraglottic devices.

questions.question: Low molecular weight heparin should be stopped before surgery:
questions.explanation: Low molecular weight heparin (LMWH) should be stopped 12 hours before (prophylactic dose).

questions.question: Klippel-Feil syndrome causes difficult intubation due to:
questions.explanation: Klippel-Feil syndrome (cervical vertebrae fusion) causes restricted neck movements.

questions.question: Temperature monitoring should be done in GA lasting more than:
questions.explanation: Temperature should be monitored in patients receiving general anesthesia >30 minutes.

questions.question: TRALI (Transfusion-Related Acute Lung Injury) manifests within:
questions.explanation: TRALI manifests as hypoxia within 6 hours of transfusion.

questions.question: Bovet got Nobel Prize (1949) for using:
questions.explanation: Bovet got Nobel Prize (1949) for using Suxamethonium (Succinylcholine) in practice.

questions.question: Diffusion hypoxia (Fink effect) occurs when:
questions.explanation: When nitrous oxide delivery is stopped, it gushes into alveoli replacing oxygen causing diffusion hypoxia.

questions.question: Nitrous oxide is absolutely contraindicated in:
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide is absolutely contraindicated in pneumothorax.

questions.question: Hagen-Poiseuille law states flow rate is proportional to fourth power of:
questions.explanation: Flow rate is directly proportional to pressure gradient and fourth power of radius of tube.

questions.question: Recurring respiratory depression is exhibited by:
questions.explanation: Recurring respiratory depression is exhibited by all pure agonists.

questions.question: Carbon monoxide production with desiccated sodalime is MAXIMUM with:
questions.explanation: Carbon monoxide production in decreasing order: Desflurane >> Isoflurane >> halothane = Sevoflurane.

questions.question: Intubation video laryngoscopy success rates after failed direct laryngoscopy:
questions.explanation: Intubation success rates of 94–99% reported for video laryngoscopy after failed direct laryngoscopy.

questions.question: INR must be less than what to consider patient for surgery after stopping Warfarin?
questions.explanation: INR must be < 1.5 to consider the patient for surgery.

questions.question: Pierre-Robin syndrome is associated with:
questions.explanation: Pierre-Robin syndrome: Hypoplastic mandible.

questions.question: Allen's test is performed to assess patency of:
questions.explanation: Allen's test should be performed to assess the patency of ulnar artery before radial artery cannulation.

questions.question: Colloids can be safely given even if there is endothelial injury like in:
questions.explanation: Colloids can be safely given even if there is endothelial injury like in ARDS or head injury.

questions.question: 16th October is celebrated as:
questions.explanation: 16th October is celebrated as World Anesthesia Day.

questions.question: Enflurane was highly:
questions.explanation: Enflurane was highly epileptogenic.

questions.question: Nitrous oxide by inhibiting vitamin B12 can produce:
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide can produce Megaloblastic anemia by inactivating vitamin B12.

questions.question: Carotid body receptors are very sensitive to:
questions.explanation: Carotid body receptors are very sensitive to hypoxia.

questions.question: Propofol contains egg lecithin increasing chances of:
questions.explanation: As propofol contains egg lecithin there are high chances of contamination of Propofol.

questions.question: Maximum permissible concentration of nitrous oxide in theater is:
questions.explanation: Maximum permissible concentration of nitrous oxide in theater is 25 parts per million (ppm).

questions.question: Maximum permissible time for which an endotracheal tube can be kept is:
questions.explanation: Maximum permissible time for which an endotracheal tube can be kept is 14 days.

questions.question: 5-HT3 antagonists are first-line for prophylaxis and treatment of:
questions.explanation: 5-HT3 antagonists are the first-line medications for prophylaxis as well as treatment of postoperative nausea and vomiting.

questions.question: Measuring dynamic parameters by arterial cannulation is most recent advancement in:
questions.explanation: Measuring dynamic parameters by arterial cannulation may be considered as one of the most recent and useful advancement in the field of monitoring.

questions.question: Massive transfusion related dilutional coagulopathies should NOT be treated:
questions.explanation: Massive transfusion related dilutional coagulopathies should not be treated prophylactically.

questions.question: Ether is the only inhalational agent that preserves:
questions.explanation: Ether is the only agent which preserves ciliary activity.

questions.question: Minimum interval mandatory between two halothane exposures is:
questions.explanation: At least an interval of 3 months is mandatory between two exposures of halothane.
